title: Sliding Window
subtitle: Reduce O(n²) brute force to O(n) — the ultimate subarray/substring pattern

nodes.label: Sliding Window
nodes.description: A window (left, right pointers) that slides over an array/string to track a subproblem efficiently

nodes.label: Fixed-Size Window
nodes.description: Window size k is constant — never changes
items: Init: compute first window of size k
Slide: add new element, remove old element
O(n) — each element added and removed once

nodes.label: Fixed Window Problems
items: Max Average Subarray of Size K (#643)
Max Sum Subarray of Size K (variant)

nodes.label: Variable-Size Window
nodes.description: Window grows and shrinks based on a constraint condition
items: Expand right pointer to include new elements
Shrink left pointer when constraint violated
Track: window state (sum, freq map, char count)
Answer: update on valid window state

nodes.label: Universal Template
nodes.description: The code template that solves all sliding window problems

nodes.label: When to Apply
nodes.description: Keywords in the problem that signal sliding window
items: 'Sum equals / at most / at least K'

nodes.label: Complexity
nodes.description: Why sliding window beats brute force
items: Brute force: O(n²) — check all subarrays
Sliding window: O(n) — each element touched twice
Space: O(1) fixed, O(k) for frequency map
Key insight: don't recompute window overlap
